sourceText: Only one who boldly shakes the roots of the system without settling for corrupt vested interests can revive the vitality of a nation.
It means the will for reform that made a declining nation powerful again by checking the powerful forces and introducing a new military system and administrative framework.
A splendid city must not simply be a display of power, but must become a creative heart where talents and merchants from all over the world gather.
It refers to the discerning eye that achieved the empire's economic and cultural golden age by moving the capital and nurturing it as a center for beautiful architecture and commerce.
Encouraging art and philosophy internally and unfolding practical diplomacy externally is the unshakeable barrier of an empire.
It shows balanced governance that opened a golden age of culture by patronizing artists and defeated enemies by forming practical alliances with external powers.

I had to endure a horrific civil war sparked by the rebellion of my own brother, Shamash-shum-ukin. Furthermore, I spent my life amidst conflict, suppressing constant uprisings across the empire and fighting a protracted war against Elam.

I ruthlessly crushed the rebellions with military might, restoring order to the empire. I then channeled my efforts into creating a lasting legacy not of destruction, but of creation and preservation, by gathering all the knowledge of Mesopotamia into my great library at Nineveh.
The wisdom of Nabu, the art of the scribe in its entirety, I have written, collated, and checked, and I have placed it in my library for the inspection of my people.
This reflects Ashurbanipal's dedication to knowledge collection and preservation, and that his library was an asset for the empire, not just a personal collection.
I am Ashurbanipal, the great king, the mighty king, king of the universe, king of Assyria, king of the four quarters of the world.
This is his standard royal titulary, asserting his vast power and divine right to rule, granted by the gods.
I can read the intricate tablets from before the flood; I can fell a lion with my bow as I can solve a complex omen.
